title: Square of numbers

content.problemStatement:
Write a program that uses a for-each loop to print the square of each element in list of NNN space separated integers, but skips elements greater than 101010.

Check the sample input and output below for further clarity.

**Note**: Output the square of each element on a new line.
constraints: All squares fit in a signed 64‑bit integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of integers. The second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the square of every integer that is less than or equal to 101010, each on its own line, in the order they appear. Do not output anything for numbers greater than 101010.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the list of numbers. We iterate over the numbers (a for‑each loop is natural in Java) and check the condition x <= 101010. If it holds we compute x*x using a 64‑bit long and append the result to the output. The algorithm is linear in the total number of elements, which easily satisfies the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·N) – each integer is examined once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.
